title: GodelAI Temporal Conflicts — Expanded v2.0
created_by: L (GodelAI CEO) — MACP v2.2 via LLM generation
description: Expanded temporal_conflicts conflict dataset for C-S-P T-Score activation (target: T=0.3-0.5)

data.title: Ulcers and Helicobacter pylori

timeline.belief: Ulcers primarily caused by stress and spicy food.
Possible bacterial involvement in ulcers; controversial.
timeline.authority: Emerging research by Marshall and Warren

timeline.belief: Most peptic ulcers caused by Helicobacter pylori infection and treated with antibiotics.

data.lesson: Scientific understanding evolves with evidence, overturning long-held assumptions.

data.title: Nature of Light: Particle vs Wave

timeline.belief: Light behaves purely as a wave.
timeline.authority: Young's Double Slit Experiment and Maxwell's equations

timeline.belief: Light exhibits dual wave-particle properties.
timeline.authority: Einstein's photoelectric effect and quantum theory development

timeline.belief: Light is quantum mechanical with wave-particle duality essential for understanding phenomena.
timeline.authority: Quantum Electrodynamics and modern physics consensus

data.lesson: Complex phenomena may require integrating seemingly contradictory models.

data.title: Dietary Fat and Heart Disease

timeline.belief: Saturated fat directly causes heart disease; low-fat diets recommended.
Relationship between fat types and heart disease risk is complex; some fats beneficial.
Saturated fat's role is nuanced; focus on overall diet quality and replacing refined carbs with healthy fats.

data.lesson: Nutritional recommendations must adapt as evidence reveals complex interactions.

data.title: Causes of Mental Illness

timeline.belief: Mental illness caused primarily by moral failing or weak character.
Biological and environmental factors both contribute to mental illness.
timeline.authority: Development of psychiatric medications and research

timeline.belief: Mental illness results from complex biopsychosocial interactions requiring integrative treatment.

data.lesson: Understanding complex conditions grows by integrating multiple perspectives.

data.title: Causes of Great Depression

timeline.belief: Great Depression caused by market failures and lack of government intervention.
timeline.authority: Classical economics and early Keynesian ideas

timeline.belief: Monetary policy mistakes and international gold standard rigidity worsened depression.
Multiple interacting causes: monetary, fiscal, structural, and policy failures combined.

data.lesson: Economic phenomena often require multifaceted explanations rather than single cause models.

data.title: Causes of the Fall of the Roman Empire

timeline.belief: Barbarian invasions solely caused the fall of Rome.
Internal decay, economic issues, and administrative failures also critical.
A complex combination of internal weaknesses, external pressures, and gradual transformation led to Rome’s fall.

data.lesson: Historical events are rarely caused by single factors but by complex interplays.

data.title: Bloodletting Practice

timeline.belief: Bloodletting cures many diseases by balancing bodily humors.
Bloodletting recognized as largely ineffective and sometimes harmful.
timeline.authority: Advances in pathology and germ theory

timeline.belief: Bloodletting is obsolete except in rare conditions like hemochromatosis.

data.lesson: Medical treatments must be continuously evaluated and discarded if ineffective or harmful.

data.title: Newtonian to Relativistic Gravity

timeline.belief: Gravity is a force acting at a distance as described by Newton’s laws.
Gravity described as curvature of spacetime in General Relativity.
timeline.authority: Albert Einstein and early experimental confirmation

timeline.belief: General Relativity is the accurate model for gravity at large scales; Newtonian approximation valid at low speeds.

data.lesson: Scientific paradigms can shift from intuitive forces to abstract concepts with predictive power.

data.title: Cholesterol and Heart Disease

timeline.belief: Dietary cholesterol directly raises blood cholesterol leading to heart disease.
timeline.authority: Early lipid hypothesis and dietary guidelines

timeline.belief: Dietary cholesterol impact varies; saturated fats and trans fats more influential.
Dietary cholesterol has minimal impact on blood cholesterol for most people; focus on overall dietary patterns.
timeline.authority: American Heart Association and nutrition science

data.lesson: Nutritional science must adapt as new evidence clarifies complex biological responses.

data.title: Learning Theory: Behaviorism to Cognitive Psychology

timeline.belief: Behaviorism explains all learning via stimulus-response without internal mental states.
Cognitive processes like memory and problem-solving important in learning.
Learning involves complex interactions of behavior, cognition, emotions, and social context.

data.lesson: Models improve by incorporating previously ignored internal processes and contexts.

data.title: Effectiveness of Monetary Policy

timeline.belief: Monetary policy is effective and predictable in controlling inflation and unemployment.
Monetary policy effects uncertain; rational expectations reduce impact.
Monetary policy effective but subject to lags, expectations, and global factors.
timeline.authority: New Keynesian consensus and central banks

data.lesson: Economic policy tools have limits and must be applied with nuanced understanding.

data.title: Causes of the American Civil War

timeline.belief: Civil War caused mainly by states' rights issues.
timeline.authority: Lost Cause narrative and early histories

timeline.belief: Slavery recognized as central cause, with states’ rights as secondary.
Slavery and economic, political, and social tensions intertwined as root causes.

data.lesson: Historical narratives evolve with societal values and broadened evidence.

data.title: Polio Transmission and Prevention

timeline.belief: Polio transmitted primarily through direct contact and respiratory droplets.
Fecal-oral route is main transmission pathway; improved sanitation crucial.
Polio primarily transmitted fecal-orally; vaccines effectively prevent spread.

data.lesson: Understanding transmission pathways is essential for effective disease control.

data.title: Atomic Model Evolution

timeline.belief: Atoms are indivisible solid spheres.
Atoms have nuclei with electrons orbiting like planets.
Quantum mechanics governs electron behavior; atoms have probabilistic electron clouds.

data.lesson: Scientific models grow in sophistication as better experimental data emerge.

data.title: Vitamin C and Common Cold

timeline.belief: Vitamin C prevents and cures the common cold.
Vitamin C may slightly reduce duration but does not prevent colds.
Vitamin C supplementation has limited benefit in cold prevention for general population.

data.lesson: Popular health claims require rigorous testing to confirm efficacy.

data.title: Nature vs Nurture in Development

timeline.belief: Human behavior and traits determined mainly by genetics (nature).
Environment (nurture) plays a dominant role; behaviorism emphasized.
Human development results from interplay between genes and environment.

data.lesson: Human traits emerge from complex interactions of biology and environment.

data.title: Role of Government in Economy

timeline.belief: Government intervention necessary for economic stability.
timeline.authority: Keynesian economics and New Deal policies

timeline.belief: Market self-regulation preferred; government intervention often harmful.
Balanced approach with government intervention used as needed to correct market failures.

data.lesson: Economic policies must be flexible to changing conditions and evidence.

data.title: Causes of World War I

timeline.belief: Germany solely responsible for causing WWI.
timeline.authority: Treaty of Versailles and Allied narratives

timeline.belief: Shared responsibility among multiple European powers due to alliances and militarism.
Complex causes including nationalism, imperialism, militarism, and systemic failures across nations.

data.lesson: Complex historical events often require multi-causal explanations.

data.title: Smoking and Lung Cancer

timeline.belief: Smoking is not linked to lung cancer.
timeline.authority: Tobacco industry and some medical authorities

timeline.belief: Strong epidemiological evidence links smoking to lung cancer.
timeline.authority: Surgeon General’s reports and scientific studies

timeline.belief: Smoking is established as primary cause of lung cancer and other diseases.

data.lesson: Public health policies evolve as scientific evidence accumulates.

data.title: Homosexuality Classification

timeline.belief: Homosexuality classified as a mental disorder.
Debate leads to removal of homosexuality as disorder; some residual stigma remains.
Homosexuality is a normal variation of human sexuality; focus on rights and mental health support.

data.lesson: Social and scientific views can evolve together in understanding human diversity.
